description_long: This handcart is constructed primarily from dark, weathered wood, featuring a flat load platform made of three open slats. The sides are reinforced with thick wooden planks, each topped with pointed, spire-like finials that give the cart a fortified appearance. Two large, solid wooden wheels are mounted on either side, secured with robust wooden axles and metal bands. The front of the cart extends into two parallel wooden handles, reinforced with metal straps for durability and ease of pulling or pushing. The overall design is utilitarian yet defensive, suggesting it could be used for transporting goods or materials in environments where protection is needed. The craftsmanship is rugged, with visible wood grain and a matte, slightly rough finish.
description: A rugged wooden handcart with open slat platform, spiked side rails, and large reinforced wheels. The cart features dual wooden handles and a fortified, medieval appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open slat platform, dual handles, and pointed side rail finials.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the large wooden wheel, thick side planks with spiked finials, and metal-reinforced handles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open slat platform and the symmetry of the spiked side rails.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with similar features to view 1, showing the wheel, side planks, and handle structure.
